sources.title: An international consensus on core reproducibility items in research
sources.version: PLOS Biology 24(4): e3003726; republished April 24, 2026; acknowledgment correction published May 7, 2026
sources.locator: Abstract; Box 1; Table 1; Methods reproducibility and computational reproducibility items. The correction changes acknowledgments, not the cited definitions.

uses.rightsBasisEvidence: Used to verify terminology variation and the roles of sufficiently described methods, code, data, and analysis conditions. Lesson prose, definitions, examples, diagram, table, prompts, and sequence are independently written; no source expression or figure is reused.

agentAccess.notes: PLOS terms were checked before substantive article access. They provide open article access and article reuse terms and contained no prohibition on this bounded agent reading. The public article and correction were accessed without login, paywall, batch retrieval, or access-control bypass.

sources.title: What is replication?
sources.version: PLOS Biology 18(3): e3000691; commissioned Perspective; not externally peer reviewed
sources.locator: Article type and editorial note; introduction; sections 'What is replication?' and 'Replication is about the outcome, not the intentions'; conclusion

uses.rightsBasisEvidence: Used to verify the same-data versus new-data distinction and the diagnostic, non-binary interpretation of replication results. All lesson expression, fictional cases, prompts, and reasoning are original; no source example, figure, or wording is reused.

agentAccess.notes: PLOS terms were checked before the public article was read and no restriction on this bounded agent access was found. Access used the public article route without authentication, paywall, automated bulk download, or access-control bypass.

sources.title: Preregistration
sources.locator: Sections 'What is preregistration?', 'Why preregister?', and guidance on changes, confirmatory analyses, and exploratory analyses

uses.rightsBasisEvidence: Used to verify preregistration as an advance plan, the separation of planned and exploratory work, and disclosure of deviations. Lesson wording, teaching structure, examples, and prompts are independently created; no source expression is reused.

agentAccess.notes: The OSF/COS public terms, updated January 10, 2025, were checked before the initiative page. They describe public website and API use and contained no agent-ingestion prohibition applicable to this bounded reading. No login, bulk collection, or technical-control bypass was used.

sources.title: Registered Reports
sources.locator: Overview and process description for Stage 1 review, in-principle acceptance, Stage 2 review, and clearly labeled exploratory analyses

uses.rightsBasisEvidence: Used to verify the general two-stage Registered Reports process. The lesson avoids absolute claims about eliminating bias, notes that journal implementation varies, and reuses no source language, visual, example, or organization.

agentAccess.notes: The OSF/COS public terms were checked before the substantive page and no applicable agent-ingestion restriction was found. The page was read through ordinary public access without login, scraping, rate-limit evasion, or access-control bypass.

sources.title: E9(R1) Statistical Principles for Clinical Trials: Addendum: Estimands and Sensitivity Analysis in Clinical Trials — Guidance for Industry
sources.locator: Sections II and V.B; Section VI, especially pages 19–22; Glossary definition of sensitivity analysis
sources.license: U.S. Government work; public domain except where otherwise noted

uses.rightsBasisEvidence: Used to verify that sensitivity analyses examine robustness to departures from assumptions and should remain aligned to the target question or estimand, with key analyses planned and assumptions documented. The lesson generalizes only this methodological principle and independently creates all language and examples.

agentAccess.notes: FDA website policies were checked before the guidance. They identify most FDA website content as public domain unless noted and contained no agent-ingestion restriction for this access. The official public PDF was read without login, automation at scale, or access-control bypass.

sources.title: Principles and Best Practices for Protecting Participant Privacy
sources.locator: Operational Principles; 'Apply Appropriate De-identification Measures'; controlled-access repositories and data use agreements
sources.license: U.S. Government work; public domain unless otherwise noted

uses.rightsBasisEvidence: Used to verify that responsible data sharing considers consent, law, de-identification, residual privacy risk, controlled access, and data-use agreements. No NIH wording, checklist, media, or case is reproduced; all course expression and scenarios are original.

agentAccess.notes: NIH public-site reuse information was checked before the guidance and states that most NIH public website content is public domain unless noted. No agent-ingestion restriction was found. The current public guidance was read without login, bulk collection, or technical-control bypass.

sources.title: Increasing Transparency Through a Multiverse Analysis
sources.locator: Abstract; introduction on data-processing choices and selective reporting; section 'The multiverse analysis'; Discussion

uses.rightsBasisEvidence: Used to verify multiverse analysis as systematic examination of reasonable data-processing choices, result dependence on those choices, and the selective-reporting concern when one preferred path is shown without the alternatives. The lesson uses only facts in original language and does not reuse the source example, analysis, data, figure, or expressive structure.

agentAccess.notes: SAGE journal terms were checked before substantive source access. The terms include text-and-data-mining conditions and no prohibition applicable to this bounded factual reading was found. Access used public bibliographic and article routes without login circumvention, bulk download, or technical-control bypass.
